title: Handoff
description: Descriptive JSON-Schema-style contract for a collab-kit handoff artifact (ARCHITECTURE.md §7.2 typed contracts). A handoff is a Markdown file: a `---`-delimited frontmatter block of simple `key: value` pairs, followed by a body of `## Section` headings. This document is DOCUMENTATION and the single source of truth that drives tools/lib/contracts.py:validate_handoff(); it is hand-rolled, NOT enforced by a jsonschema library (stdlib-only project). Keep the two in lockstep.

frontmatter.description: Parsed key/value pairs from the leading `---`-fenced block.

id.description: Stable handoff id (e.g. '001'). Load-bearing for handoff-loss edges (§7.4).

date.description: ISO-8601 date the handoff was issued.

guardrails.description: Optional bracketed list, e.g. [concurrency, path-safety]. Each entry is a machine-checkable explicit constraint (§7.4).

sections.description: Body `## Heading` -> section text. Names are the heading text verbatim.

Summary.description: Required. What this handoff is and why.

Constraints.description: Optional but PREFERRED. Declared, typed constraints as `- [ID] text` bullets (e.g. `- [C1] paper-trading only`). IDs are stable and identity-addressable, so handoff_loss (§7.4) measures retention by ID across a revision edge rather than scraping prose. Declaring constraints here is what makes handoff loss a trustworthy number.

x-constraint-sources.description: Where constraints are read for handoff_loss (§7.4). The DECLARED path is preferred and identity-addressed; the scraped path is a lossy legacy fallback used only when no `## Constraints` section is present.
x-constraint-sources.declared: `## Constraints` bullets of the form `- [ID] text` -> {id: text} (preferred, matched by ID)

scraped_fallback.imperative_lines: any body line containing a must/required/MUST imperative
